title: FAROO Proof of Concept Demo - Legal Service Desk & Notion Integration
summary: A proof-of-concept demo was presented to the FAROO prospect showcasing a legal service desk platform with AI-powered specialist routing (via OpenAI). The client currently operates using Notion for case management, specialist coordination, billing, and CRM — all manually maintained. Two complementary workstreams were identified: commercial/sales process automation and operational process automation. The agreed next step is to identify specific manual Notion workflows that can be automated through agentic flows while preserving the client's existing organizational structure.

discussion_points.description: A barebones proof-of-concept was demonstrated featuring specialist management (individuals and boutiques), client management, case creation with legal domains/jurisdictions/budgets, and AI-powered specialist assignment using OpenAI. The system includes Kanban-style dashboards for case tracking and workload visibility (e.g., showing specialists at capacity in red).

The client showed their existing Notion setup which handles commercial process tracking, operational case management, specialist/lawyer database, document structure, referral commissions, billing/payment tracking, and invoicing. Notion is valued for its flexibility ('lienzo'/canvas) but everything is manual. They pay 57 hours/month and lawyers access as free guests.

Two distinct processes were identified: (1) Commercial/Sales — prospect acquisition via LinkedIn, pre-meeting qualification, growth engine, and CRM capabilities that Notion lacks; (2) Operational — case assignment, specialist coordination, case tracking, billing. Both are complementary but require different solutions.

Key pain points identified: manual data entry in Notion, WhatsApp message tracking (not knowing if messages were answered), manual invoice handling (creating in Alegra, downloading, uploading to Notion), manual case status updates, and lack of prospecting/campaign capabilities.

The client expressed strong interest in having WhatsApp and email channels automatically feed information into the system, enabling automated responses for routine queries (e.g., case status updates like 'the appeal has been filed') and flagging items requiring personal attention.

Rather than replacing Notion entirely, the team proposed identifying automation points where the service desk platform can run underneath and feed Notion, or integrate bidirectionally. The client's Notion AI assistant ('el narizon') already generates proposals and connects information, making a hybrid approach attractive.

The consulting team proposed prioritizing automation like an investment portfolio: short-term (automate biggest pain points now for immediate ROI), medium-term, and long-term improvements. Start with what causes the most headaches today.

The technical team proposed using agentic workflows to handle administrative/operational tasks like creating cases, assigning specialists, and transferring data — shifting the client's role from 'doing the assignment' to 'reviewing the assignment'.

action_items.description: Identify and document specific manual processes in Notion that transfer information to/from other systems (Alegra invoicing, Google Drive, WhatsApp, email) as candidates for automation
Map out the three core processes: sales/commercial process, operational/case management process, and billing/invoicing process
Build a more involved proof of concept targeting one specific Notion workflow, demonstrating an agentic flow that automates data transfer between Notion and another system
Review the summary document previously sent via email and confirm pain points are accurately captured

decisions: The service desk concept is validated as the right operational model for FAROO's legal case management needs
The project will be split into two workstreams: commercial/sales process and operational process
Notion will not be immediately replaced; instead, the approach will focus on automating manual processes and integrating with the existing Notion setup
Prioritization will follow a short-term/medium-term/long-term investment approach, starting with the highest-pain manual processes
The next POC iteration should target a specific manual Notion workflow for automation via agentic flows
next_steps: Client to walk through a specific manual process (Notion to external system data transfer) so the team can evaluate agentic flow automation potential
Team to design a more involved POC demonstrating automation of one identified manual Notion workflow
Map all integration points: Notion, Alegra (invoicing), Google Drive (documents), WhatsApp, and email
Define automation priorities using the short/medium/long-term investment framework
Explore WhatsApp and email ingestion as automatic data sources for the service desk platform

diagrams.title: Current State: FAROO Manual Operations
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Channels["Input Channels"]
        WA["WhatsApp"]
        EM["Email"]
        REF["Referrals"]
    end
    subgraph Notion["Notion Workspace"]
        CRM["CRM / Commercial\nProcess"]
        CASES["Operational\nCase Management"]
        LAWYERS["Lawyer\nDatabase"]
        BILLING["Billing &\nPayment Tracking"]
        DOCS["Document\nStructure"]
        AI["Notion AI Assistant"]
    end
    subgraph External["External Systems"]
        ALEGRA["Alegra\nInvoicing"]
        GDRIVE["Google Drive\nDocuments"]
    end
    WA -->|"Manual Entry"| CASES
    EM -->|"Manual Entry"| CASES
    REF -->|"Manual Entry"| CRM
    CRM -->|"Manual"| CASES
    CASES -->|"Manual Link"| LAWYERS
    CASES -->|"Manual Link"| DOCS
    DOCS -->|"Manual Link"| GDRIVE
    CASES -->|"Manual"| BILLING
    BILLING -->|"Manual Upload"| ALEGRA
    AI -->|"Proposals"| CRM
    style Channels fill:#ff9800,color:#fff
    style Notion fill:#2196f3,color:#fff
    style External fill:#4caf50,color:#fff

diagrams.title: Proposed Architecture: Legal Service Desk with AI Routing
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart LR
    subgraph Ingestion["Data Ingestion Layer"]
        WA["WhatsApp"]
        EM["Email"]
        WEB["Web Portal"]
    end
    subgraph ServiceDesk["Service Desk Platform"]
        TICKET["Ticket\nCreation"]
        AI_ROUTE["AI Specialist\nRouting\n(OpenAI)"]
        KANBAN["Kanban\nDashboard"]
        WORKLOAD["Workload\nScoring"]
    end
    subgraph Masters["Master Data"]
        SPEC["Specialists\n(Individuals & Boutiques)"]
        CLIENTS["Clients"]
        JURIS["Jurisdictions"]
        DOMAINS["Legal Domains"]
    end
    subgraph Integration["Integration Layer"]
        NOTION["Notion\nSync"]
        ALEGRA2["Alegra\nInvoicing"]
        GDRIVE2["Google Drive"]
    end
    WA --> TICKET
    EM --> TICKET
    WEB --> TICKET
    TICKET --> AI_ROUTE
    AI_ROUTE -->|"Match"| SPEC
    AI_ROUTE --> KANBAN
    SPEC --> WORKLOAD
    CLIENTS --> TICKET
    JURIS --> AI_ROUTE
    DOMAINS --> AI_ROUTE
    KANBAN --> NOTION
    KANBAN --> ALEGRA2
    KANBAN --> GDRIVE2
    style Ingestion fill:#ff9800,color:#fff
    style ServiceDesk fill:#1565c0,color:#fff
    style Masters fill:#7b1fa2,color:#fff
    style Integration fill:#2e7d32,color:#fff

diagrams.title: AI Specialist Assignment Flow
diagrams.mermaid_code:
sequenceDiagram participant C as Client/Channel participant SD as Service Desk participant AI as OpenAI Engine participant DB as Specialist DB participant S as Assigned Specialist C->>SD: New case request SD->>SD: Create ticket with metadata Note over SD: Legal domain, jurisdiction,
priority, budget, deadline SD->>AI: Send case requirements AI->>DB: Query specialist profiles DB-->>AI: Expertise, workload, rates,
jurisdiction match AI->>AI: Score and rank candidates AI-->>SD: Recommended specialist
(e.g., Carlos Andres Mejia) SD->>SD: Display recommendation SD->>S: Assign case S-->>SD: Accept & generate work plan SD->>SD: Update Kanban dashboard

diagrams.title: Commercial vs Operational Process Flow
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart TD
    START["Prospect Arrives\n(LinkedIn, Web, Referral)"] --> QUAL["Pre-Meeting\nQualification"]
    QUAL --> PREP["Prepared Meeting\nwith Context"]
    PREP --> DISCUSS["Discussion &\nProposal"]
    DISCUSS --> ACCEPT{"Client\nAccepts?"}
    ACCEPT -->|"No"| NURTURE["Nurture &\nFollow Up"]
    NURTURE --> QUAL
    ACCEPT -->|"Yes"| DEPLOY["Deploy Operations"]
    subgraph Commercial["COMMERCIAL PROCESS"]
        QUAL
        PREP
        DISCUSS
        ACCEPT
        NURTURE
    end
    subgraph Operational["OPERATIONAL PROCESS"]
        DEPLOY --> CREATE["Create Case\nin Service Desk"]
        CREATE --> ASSIGN["AI Assigns\nSpecialist"]
        ASSIGN --> TRACK["Track Progress\nvia Kanban"]
        TRACK --> BILL["Billing &\nInvoicing"]
        BILL --> CLOSE["Case\nClosure"]
    end
    style Commercial fill:#1565c0,color:#fff
    style Operational fill:#2e7d32,color:#fff

diagrams.title: Agentic Automation Integration with Notion
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Triggers["Event Triggers"]
        T1["New WhatsApp Message"]
        T2["New Email Received"]
        T3["Invoice Created in Alegra"]
        T4["Case Status Change"]
    end
    subgraph Agentic["Agentic Flow Engine"]
        DETECT["Detect Event"]
        CLASSIFY["Classify &\nRoute"]
        EXECUTE["Execute\nAutomation"]
        REVIEW["Queue for\nHuman Review"]
    end
    subgraph Actions["Automated Actions"]
        A1["Update Notion\nCase Record"]
        A2["Auto-Reply to\nStatus Inquiry"]
        A3["Upload Invoice\nto Notion"]
        A4["Notify Specialist"]
        A5["Update Billing\nTracker"]
    end
    T1 --> DETECT
    T2 --> DETECT
    T3 --> DETECT
    T4 --> DETECT
    DETECT --> CLASSIFY
    CLASSIFY -->|"Routine"| EXECUTE
    CLASSIFY -->|"Requires Judgment"| REVIEW
    EXECUTE --> A1
    EXECUTE --> A2
    EXECUTE --> A3
    EXECUTE --> A4
    EXECUTE --> A5
    REVIEW -->|"Approved"| EXECUTE
    style Triggers fill:#ff9800,color:#fff
    style Agentic fill:#1565c0,color:#fff
    style Actions fill:#2e7d32,color:#fff
